summary: Chrome MV3 extension popup tested via standalone http://localhost:8888/Popup/popup.html. 35/35 UI assertions passed (master toggle gating, performance segmented control with auto-detect hint, skin picker render + 6 previews, 5 glow chips with EMBER/FROST/NEON/LEGEND/HALO labels + deselect, intensity slider 10%-150%, mutually-exclusive glow target, 5 board theme swatches exclusive selection, particles/cracks toggles independent, tournament + import toasts, full state persistence via localStorage shim across reload). Static review of /app/content.js (639 lines) passed: node --check OK, QUALITY low/medium/high presets present, detectQuality() uses navigator.deviceMemory + hardwareConcurrency, .piece.dragging override generated in applyEffect glow CSS, chrome.storage.onChanged handles ALL 10 storage keys, MutationObserver only does class diff (no getBoundingClientRect inside its callback — layout reads are confined to fxAttach setup and ResizeObserver), tournament mode picks random sessionSkin once per page load, FPS probe runs once and downgrades auto quality. manifest.json is valid MV3 (single content script content.js, all 3 icons exist, web_accessible_resources scoped to chess.com). No references to legacy files content-script.js / styles.css / style.css in any code (only one mention of 'content-script.js' inside design_guidelines.json which is a docs/notes file — not loaded by the extension).
critical_code_review_comments: design_guidelines.json (line 87) still contains a stale reference to the deleted 'content-script.js'. Not loaded by the extension (manifest only references content.js) so harmless, but the doc should be cleaned up to avoid confusion for future contributors.

popup.js is 349 lines and content.js is 639 lines — both under the 700-line guideline but content.js is getting close; consider splitting fx engine (lines ~230-506) into its own module if more features are added.

applyEffect (content.js:165) recomputes glow on every storage change; fine for popup-driven changes but if intensity is dragged rapidly via the live setGlowIntensity message path, consider rAF-throttling. Currently acceptable.

popup.js detectQuality (line 74) duplicates content.js detectQuality (line 82). Minor DRY violation; in a browser extension with no shared module system this is the pragmatic choice but worth a comment noting the two must stay in sync.

No data-testid on the perf-desc paragraph or intensity-value span — they were assertable via id selectors so not blocking, but for stricter future tests adding data-testid='perf-desc' and 'intensity-value' would help.
seed_data_creation: None. localStorage was cleared at test start and exercised by the test itself for the persistence check.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static file server: cd /app && python3 -m http.server 8888 (already running). Open http://localhost:8888/Popup/popup.html at viewport width 420 to mirror real popup. popup.js has an isExt guard (line 9) with a localStorage shim so all storage and event logic works outside the extension. content.js cannot run in the preview (needs chrome.runtime + chess.com DOM) — review statically only. All interactive elements have data-testid attributes; see popup.html for the canonical list. Inspect /app/test_reports/iteration_1.json for the full list of selectors exercised.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Implementation matches the spec end-to-end: performance-aware quality presets, beautiful dark-theme popup with full state persistence, no legacy file references, valid MV3 manifest, and a content script that respects layout-read constraints (no getBoundingClientRect inside MutationObserver) and applies a .piece.dragging override to keep dragging frames cheap.
